raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1485 strengthens the enforcement of state housing laws by granting the Attorney General the unconditional, statutory right to intervene in any pending third party actions brought against local governments for violating state housing laws.",
    "The Attorney General has made housing a top priority for the Department of Justice.",
    "California is currently facing a housing crisis.",
    "Most of California’s state housing laws include a 'private right of action' that allows third parties to file lawsuits when local governments break housing laws.",
    "Current law requires the Attorney General to petition the court to intervene in cases involving violations of state housing laws.",
    "AB 1485 would allow the Attorney General to represent the State’s interests in third-party lawsuits without unnecessary delay.",
    "California needs to build 2.5 million homes by 2031.",
    "Empowering the Attorney General’s office to enforce state housing laws ensures accountability of cities for building their fair share of housing."
  ]
}
```
